Specialist Suicide Bereavement service for Kent and Medway. This is NOT specifically a counselling service, but will signpost to counselling as required and have their own  in house counselling. The service provides bespoke practical, emotional and social support in the aftermath of a suicide, with issues such as funeral arrangements, financial impacts, attending coroners inquests, dealing with media and anything else that may impact a family or individual by a sudden traumatic loss to suicide.

The service is open to anyone affected by a suicide either recent or historic and is open to colleagues, neighbours, schools, friends and family members, with the aim of reducing the distress and further ripple effect that a death by suicide can cause.

The aim of the Outreach Service is to provide high-quality support and advice to enable students with SEND to succeed in the mainstream setting within Nurseries, Primary and Secondary schools.

Description:
Marlborough Outreach Team is a team of specialist teachers who are able to support children with ASD, Communication and Interaction Needs within the mainstream school setting. Based at the Marlborough Centre, a provision for children with a diagnosis of ASD, we are able to support children in mainstream schools and nurseries with a diagnosis of ASD andor communication and interaction needs. We provide support to children to enable them to access the curriculum and reach their full potential through providing training to school staff and working directly with children to address the difficulties that they may have which act as a barrier to their learning. We provide training in a variety of areas such as Autism Awareness and Challenging Behaviour related to Autism, but are equally able to provide a training package bespoke to meet your needs. Feel free to contact us for more information.
Eligibilityreferral criteria:
- ASD diagnosis
- Communication and Interaction Needs
- Referral through the pupils school SENCO
Referral Process: using the form on the website; https://hoo-st-werburgh.medway.sch.uk/marlborough-centre/outreach/meet-the-team

Our work in schools, colleges, universities and other educational settings includes training and consultancy for staff; one-to-one mentoring for young trans, non-binary and gender questioning young people; and workshops, lectures and assemblies for students. Description: A mental health service designed to specifically support members of the LGBTQ community. Provides advice and support as well as an online instant messaging service which is confidential, non judgemental and anonymous.

At Stonewall, we stand for lesbian, gay, bi, trans, queer, questioning and ace (LGBTQ) people everywhere. We imagine a world where all LGBTQ people are free to be themselves and we can live our lives to the full. We are part of a vibrant global movement for change made up of LGBTQ people, our allies, families and friends. Since day one, we've fought for freedom, for equity and for potential. 

Over the last 30 years, we have helped create transformative change in the lives of LGBTQ people in the UK. Today, we have equal rights to love, marry and have children, and our lives, families and relationships are represented as part of the national curriculum in most of the UK.  Our campaigns drive positive change in public attitudes and public policy. We ensure LGBTQ people can thrive throughout our lives by building deep, sustained change programmes with the institutions that have the biggest impact on us, whether were learning, working, praying or playing sport. We make sure that the world hears and learns from LGBTQ communities, and our work is grounded in evidence and expertise. And were committed to empowering people to create change in their own communities.  

We face rising intolerance degrading our hard-won rights. But we won't be silenced. Not until all of us are free to be proud, free to be loved, free to be together, free to be who we are. Our work continues until the world we imagine is the world we live in.

We provide information  advice for individuals, schoolscolleges and workplaces

Description:
Every child and family is entitled to five health checks. In addition, we offer a pre-school entry review. The health visiting team offer packages of care when families require extra advice and support. The packages offered include maternal mental health; relationship issues; children with additional needs, feeding issues and nutrition, child development, childrens sleep, toileting and behaviour. We work closely with midwives, GPs, Paediatricians, local childrens centres and voluntary organisations to provide you with the best possible care. Our health visiting team provide advice and support on:
Safe sleep, toilet training, managing tantrums, safety to reduce accidents in the home and child health.
General parenting support. 
Family health and minor illnesses.
Growth, physical and emotional development. 
Importance of play, stimulation and learning through play.
Specific support on subjects such as postnatal depression and parental emotional wellbeing
Advice on immunisations and national screening programmes
Advice on family health and minor illnesses
Breastfeeding, introducing solids and health eating.
In addition, we offer healthy child clinics and postnatal groups. 
Support parents and refer onto relevant services.
Referral Process: If you would like to talk to a health visitor, please contact the duty line on 0300 123 3444, and choose option 4 between Monday to Friday 9am-5pm.

Concat: Medway Council: Medway Men in Sheds
Description: Medway men in sheds serves as vital sanctuary significantly contributing to the improvement of mens health and the well-being aimed at those not in work. Within the walls of these free shed sessions 5 days a week, tailored by the men to suit different interests, men find a haven for purposeful engagement, social interaction, and skill development. Studies have consistently shown that active participation in mens sheds leads to enhanced mental health, reduced stress levels, and a sense of belonging among members. As a result, these positive effects ripple through the wider community, promoting healthier opportunities, enabling positive changes for people and planet and alleviating the strain on primary healthcare services, including GP waiting times.

Description: The children we work with have often been affected by the harshest personal circumstances, having been traumatised by experiences including physical or sexual abuse, domestic violence, or neglect.  The emotional landscape for these children is often bleak, their difficulties culminating repeated exclusions or refusal to engage at school, and a breakdown in relationships at home.  They are often extremely isolated, and as a result of emotional trauma may be experiencing aggressive or withdrawn behaviour, or suffer from depression, anxiety or low self-esteem.  Referred children sometimes also have diagnoses of physical or emotional disorders including ADHD or Autistic Spectrum Disorders. Trauma itself has a dramatic effect on key areas of development in early childhood, leading to under-developed physical and sensory abilities and motor skills, or eating disorders.  We work with children to help them overcome these issues. NOTE: see financial contribution required
Support Category: Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Trauma/Victim support; Behaviour; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: Crisis team for Children and Young People
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Service: Crisis team for Children and Young People
Description:
The NELFT Kent and Medway Crisis and Home Treatment Team offer short term intervention for young people who are presenting in mental health crisis. Our aim is to prevent admission to tier 4 services by seeing young people in the community for a short period of time alongside the locality teams.  
We assess young people who present to AE or have been referred from their locality teams with significant mental health concerns, and we offer short term support for several weeks in the community following their presentation. We can see young people in AE, locality teams, homes, schools, or the local community if preferred by the young person. Our contacts are either face to face or via telephone. 
If a young person requires admission to tier 4 services, the crisis team will support the young person until a bed has been identified. 
The crisis team also support young people who have been detained on a section 136 and are currently placed in our health based place of safety.
Referral Process:
Via NELFT locality teams (across Kent and Medway) � Internal
Via NELFT single point of access or mental health direct � internal 
Via acute trusts (A&E or acute wards)� 0300 555 1080

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: Kent & Medway Complex Autism Service (KAMCAS)
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Description: Tertiary specialist service commissioned to support adults with complex presentations and a confirmed diagnosis of autism where other professionals within the mental health and the care system are unable to meet the persons needs effectively.
Support Category: Behaviour; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: Medway Young Person's wellbeing service
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Description:
The Medway Young Persons' Wellbeing Service provides emotional wellbeing and mental health advice and support for young people and their families.

The Medway Young Persons' Wellbeing Service offers advice and support for:
 stress
 low mood 
 depression
 anxiety
 self-harm 
 difficult to manage behaviours as well as support for neurodevelopmental difficulties such as ADHD or ASD.

The way in which support is offered differs from young person to young person depending on the type and severity of concern. For some young people their emotional well-being may be having a significant impact on their day to day life and in these cases the YPWS may offer a short period of face to face or virtual support either in 1:1 sessions or in a group. All NELFTs direct support options aim for young people to meet their own goals. These include: online selfhelp; sign-posting to other organisations; crisis support; specialist assessments; face to face or virtual therapy either individually or in groups
Young people who have been discharged can refer themselves back into the service at any time.
Support Category: Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Self harm; Behaviour; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: Mental Health direct 24/7
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Description: If you currently receive a service from a team at NELFT, such as a local community mental health team or a psychiatrist, then make contact with them as soon as you can. It may be helpful to refer to your care plan, which should have details of who to contact in an emergency. If it is out of office hours or you dont know who to contact, you should call NELFT Mental health direct. You can call for mental health help and advice anytime.
Support Category: Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Self harm; Behaviour; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Eating Disorder; Health; Diagnosed mental health e.g. OCD, Psychosis, Personality Disorder

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: MIND fresh
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Description: Online self help resource for CYP and parents
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Bullying; Trauma/Victim support; Behaviour;  Self harm; Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Eating Disorder; Bereavement; Family Support; Wellbeing

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: Neurodevelopmental & Learning Disability Services
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Description:
The Neurodevelopmental and Learning Disability Service for Kent and Medway is a specialist service consisting of highly skilled multidisciplinary team responsible for the assessment and diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders including Autism Spectrum Condition (ASC), Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), Learning Disabilities (LD) and Tics and Tourettes syndrome (TS).

The Learning Disability team is a community based specialist service offering support to children and young people, aged between 0-18 years, who are experiencing significant mental health problems and who are diagnosed with moderate to severe learning disability.
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: Open Road
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Description:
Open Road is a drug and alcohol recovery support charity. It provides services to support individuals on their recovery from drug and alcohol addiction.

They have a specialised Young People Service who provide honest information about how drugs and alcohol work, what the risks are and how to reduce those risks. You do not have to want to stop using to start working with them.

They can also help if you are worried about a friend or a family member and their drink and drug use.

They also have days out including Go-Ape, paintballing, the zoo, and activities such as trampolining and bowling.

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: Single Point of Access - SPA
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Description: Provides consultation around mental health support and signposts to suggested support
Support Category: Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Self harm; Behaviour; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Diagnosed mental health e.g. OCD, Psychosis, Personality Disorder

Concat: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust: Tier 4 Inpatient interventions
Provider: Kent and Medway Mental Health NHS Trust
Description: covers facilities for children and young people with mental health problems who require hospital admission. Children can be placed in a tier 4 unit either as a voluntary (informal) patient, or as a person who has been detained under the Mental Health Act 1983.
Referral Process: Referral from a tier 3 CAMHS intervention service
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Trauma/Victim support; Behaviour;  Self harm; Anxiety/Depression/Low mood;  Eating Disorder; Diagnosed mental health e.g. OCD, Psychosis, Personality Disorder

Description: Can provide advice  guidance around sexual health. Services include supporting sexual problems which may be as a result of a psychological problem, sexual assault referral centre, abortion support etc

Concat: NHS England: NHS Young People�s Health Challenge (YPHC)
Description: a new toolkit to help 714-year-olds across England to find out more about the NHS. The toolkit helps children and young people to increase their health literacy, create aspirations to work or volunteer within the NHS and reduce health inequalities
Age range: Primary school age and Secondary School age

Description: The Solihull Approach is a not-for-profit NHS service for practitioners and parents to support emotional health and wellbeing in children, families, adults and older adults. The sound and well-researched ideas developed by a team of clinical psychologists that underpin the Approach are embedded in every aspect of our training.
Referral Process: Medway families can now access some courses on the Solihull website �www.inourplace.co.uk� �for free. The courses are based on the Solihull Approach. An individual can �register on the site and then access the courses using the codeword�Invicta.

Concat: NHS Kent and Medway: Kent & Medway Recovery and Wellbeing College
Service: Kent & Medway Recovery and Wellbeing College
Description:
The Kent  Medway Recovery  Wellbeing College provides health related educational courses for adults living in Kent  Medway, free of charge,  
in supportive and accessible learning spaces.  Through education, people can make sense of their experiences and learn how to take care of themselves and others.

Concat: NHS Kent and Medway: Kent & Medway Safe Havens
Description:
Kent and Medway Safe Havens offers out of hours mental health support for anyone aged 16 if you are feeling distressed, frightened and overwhelmed. We are a non-clinical alternative to AE who provide a drop-in service open to anyone and need crisis metal health support, emotional wellbeing support or anyone who is suffering with anxiety or any other mental health symptoms. 

The service is open from 6pm to 11pm including weekends and bank holidays.
Referral Process: Whilst professionals can make referrals, a referral is not needed for individuals to access Safe Havens. Call: 07850 901 151 or 07808 795 036 or Email: Medway.mhm@nhs.net

Description: an online safe, anonymous and supportive community which offers counselling, discussion boards, help articles, journal and self-help tools for 10-25 year olds
Age range: Primary school age, Secondary School age and post 16

Concat: NHS Kent and Medway: Live Well Kent and Medway
Description: Live Well Kent and Medway offers support with keeping active and healthy and everyday living including: managing your money, debt and benefits, housing support and guidance, and accessing counselling.
Support Category: Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Wellbeing; Homelessness, finance, employment; Bullying; Behaviour; Family Support; Health

Concat: NHS Kent and Medway: Medway Talking therapies
Description:
Everyone goes through difficult times; sometimes our problems affect our day-to-day lives and we feel that we cant cope. If you are in this situation, you are not alone.

Medway Talking Therapies is a FREE, confidential service, commissioned by the NHS, and available to anyone aged 18 or over and who is registered with a GP in Medway.
Referral Process:
Call us � 0333 091 0141 (local rate)
Email us � medway@insighthealthcare.org. 
Ask your GP/other professional supporting you to refer you or use the online form; https://www.insighthealthcare.org/our-services/talking-therapies/find-a-service/medway-talking-therapies/#selfReferForm

Description: QWELL provides anonymous online counselling and online resources to those aged 18 years who reside in Medway. The service will provide an early response to, and identification of emotional wellbeing and mental health problems, leading to improved well-being, prevention of symptom escalation and provision of the right care at the right time.
Referral Process: see weblink for how to access
Support Category: Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Homelessness, finance, employment; Family Support; Wellbeing

Concat: NHS Kent and Medway: Service User Network (SUN)
Description:
A community network of peer-support groups for people who are experiencing difficulties with complex emotions often associated with Personality Disorder, but you dont need a diagnosis to attend. SUN groups can support someone by helping them to share their experiences with others who are feeling the same, or similar. These groups are for people who find their emotions are affecting their relationships, or have difficulty managing their emotions.

The aim of SUN is to support someone in the community by connecting them with like-minded people who are feeling the same. This helps someone to feel less isolated and can help them to manage their feelings before they reach the point where they cant cope. SUN groups are led by a facilitator with Lived Experience and a clinician facilitator.
Referral Process: Register your interest in SUN by emailing SUN.kentandmedway@nhs.net
Support Category: Diagnosed mental health e.g. OCD, Psychosis, Personality Disorder

Concat: NHS Kent and Medway: SHOUT/Release the pressure
Description: helpline consists of a team of highly trained and experienced support workers who provide emotional guidance and information

Concat: North Kent Mind: Wellbeing services and resources
Description: offer you a range of wellbeing services and resources if you are going through a difficult time or struggling emotionally. This includes courses to develop mental health coping strategies, help you manage depression, anxiety and stress, and to improve your self-esteem and become more confident. There is also employment support if you feel that your mental health has become a barrier to finding work.
Support Category: Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Wellbeing; Homelessness, finance, employment

Description:
Building Connections is an online service for young people up to the age of 19, empowering them to find a way through loneliness.
Young people work with a trained befriender, who guides them and champions them each step of the way. Building Connections gives young people tools that can help them build their confidence and better equip them to manage loneliness.
Referral Process: Refer to the make a referral section on website for guidelines and instructions
Age range: Primary school age, Secondary School age and post 16

Description: Offers interventions to support those impacted by domestic abuse or dysfunctional controlling relationships, and the psychological trauma and practical fall out resulting from this on family life. Offer emotional and practical solutions and where necessary safety planning strategies. Focused behavioural support will also be offered to children who may have witnessed and been traumatised by Domestic Abuse.
Source: commissioned by Glen Page and Approved by the group

Description: One Stop Shops are a place for anyone who is experiencing domestic violence to visit. The atmosphere is non-judgmental and informal, all visits are anonymous, and you can visit as many times as necessary.
Referral Process: held at the Sunlight Centre, 105 Richmond Road, Gillingham every Tuesday morning 9.30am-11.30am

Description: Bemix is an organisation that aims to make sure we live in a world where every single person is valued. Bemix provides group projects and internships to ensure that people with and without learning difficulties or autism are working and learning together. Their group projects allow people of any ability or background to develop their skills and make friends. Their supported internships are year-long programmes aimed at people aged 17 to 24 with an Education, Health and Care Plan (EHCP).They also deliver presentations and workshops to schools to challenge negative attitudes about learning disabilityautism
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Description: Youth support workers who provide short term support for young people who find themselves in AE because they are struggling with self-harm or feeling suicidal. 7pm  11pm Monday to Thursday
Referral Process: call on 07983136215 or ask a member of the hospital staff to call or email: hellomedway@emergeadvocacy.com
Age range: Primary school age, Secondary School age and post 16

Concat: Other Services: K&M Domestic abuse support services
Description: provides a central, comprehensive resource of all Domestic Abuse related services within Kent and Medway for victims, friends  family and perpetrators
Referral Process: 0808 168-9111 or talk via Live Chat in the website

Concat: Other Services: Kent Association for the Blind
Description: Offers personalised support to families and children. They provide a range of activities and therapeutic services.
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Concat: Other Services: Medway Parents and Carers Forum
Description: Forum set up by parentscarers of children with SEND, to give first-hand experiences, peer support and advice about supporting a young person with additional needs.  Also offer support for Parents and carers with Form filling including completing DLA, PIP and Universal Credit forms
Referral Process: Self-refer by joining their forum on the website or by finding them on Facebook. You can also email info@medwaypcf.org.uk for more information or to join the forum.
Support Category: Behaviour; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Family Support; Social Care

Description: Megan CIC has a range of support groups run by people who have experienced mental health issues (and may still experience them). This includes a support group for people who have experienced depression, anxiety and bipolar, life after addition and support around emotional wellbeing in relation to neurodiversity.
Support Category: Diagnosed mental health e.g. OCD, Psychosis, Personality Disorder
Source: Approved by the group and recommended by DfE

Description: services including advocacy and children's rights, mentoring and befriending

The overall aim of Positive Behaviour Support is to help improve the quality of life for the individual and those around them. It is an evidence based approach to supporting people who may or may not be displaying behaviours of concern. It seeks to udnerstand the reasons for behaviour so that unmet needs can be met. It is a proacive and preventative approach, focussing on teaching new skills to help individuals participate.

Some schools have taken part in different levels of training including awareness or practitioner. To access internal Medway Council staff can sign up to attend through i-share. For parents, carers and residential settings please email ctuckerkentautistic.com. For schools please access through Small Steps. For more information please email diane.weekesmedway.gov.uk
Referral Process: Awareness level training available for free online. Internal staff can access through i-share
Support Category: Behaviour; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Concat: Policy Centre for African Peoples: Hope After Covid Program
Description: Hope after Covid is a Medway and Swale free coaching and engagement service inspired by African philosophy. The aim is to improve the wellbeing, mental health and social engagement of all participants 18 and over.

Concat: Policy Centre for African Peoples: Youth Fellowship Innovation Program
Description:
The Youth Innovation Fellowship Programme is a coaching and personal development project that the Policy Centre for African Peoples (PCAP) will be running until April 2025  to coach and support disadvantaged young people aged between 18 and 25 who are based outside London. All youths enrolled in the Youth Innovation Fellowship Programme will receive a free, 1 to 1 support package 
over 4 months. The support package will consist of 1 coaching session lasting 1 hour every month. The 
coaching will be personalised and tailored to the specific needs of each youth.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: A Better Medway Schools Award
Description: Award based on evidence based approaches that promote the link between good health, behaviour and achievement around 8 key areas; PSHE, Staff wellbeing, Mental Health  Emotional Wellbeing, Parent Engagement, Healthy Eating  Nutrition, Community Engagement, Physical Activity  Active Travel, Smoke Free. Using a whole school approach involving whole school community. Bronze level award schools benefit from; a free whole school membership to the PSHE Association and priority on training courses, Silver; 3x assemblies a year on health and wellbeing, 100 Health Edco voucher, extra places on YMHFA, 2 parent health promotion workshops, Gold; bespoke training coursesYMHFA delivered, 200 Health Edco voucher

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: A Better Medway website
Description: website with useful information around improving physical and emotional health and wellbeing

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Abusive Relationships
Description: lessons to enable children and young people to recognise and expect high quality  loving relationships

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: ACEs and Trauma informed practice
Description: The aim of the programme is to develop understanding about the impact of trauma on child development and learn how to recognise its effects on CYP and their behaviour, as well as how to effectively respond to its effects without causing additional trauma.  There are 3 options for training; level 1 is aimed at the wider children's workforce including lunchtime supervisors, school governors and caretakers for example. Level 2 is for staff in pastoral roles who are supporting CYP with their emotional health and wellbeing needs and level 3 is a train the trainer course. Anyone who wishes to do level 2 or 3 needs to have completed previous levels first.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Alumina facilitator training
Description: This 2 day course equips facilitators with the skills and resources to deliver a 6-7 week course for YP aged 14-19 years to support them in understanding and manager their self-harm

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Alumina online
Description: A free online 7-week course for YP aged 14-19 years to support them in understanding and manage their self-harm. Run by friendly counsellors and volunteer youth workers.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Connect 5 Training
Description: Mental health training programme drawing on CBT to help promote self-help. Train the Trainer also available

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Gangs
Description: Pack to help teachers explore reasons why children and young people may join a gang and implications and how to get support

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Interactive Safeguarding Resources
Description: Resources to help understand and identify CSE, extremism  radicalisation. Once trained individuals will be able to use the tool with young people

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Managing Family Conflicts
Description: Set of materials to address conflict within family  personal relationships and support children and young people to explore safe conflict resolution behaviours

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Mental Health Leads Partnership
Description: Medway Council's Child Health Team and Partnership Commissioning lead on a network of professionals whom work withchildren  young people.The network brings together mental health services and school settings in a collaborative place to create a whole system approach to support children and young peoples mental health and wellbeing in education settings. The group includes a collective resource platform and further opportunities to share best practice and network and keep up to date with service provision as well as policy and legislative changes. The network can support any trouble shooting or common themes that may be arising for your setting, seeking collaborative solutions and embedding a no wrong door approach for children and young people in.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Newsletter
Description: The child health team circulate various newsletters sharing useful information and resources to help support professionals to support CYP including the PSHE newsletter, recovery bulletin, child safeguarding newsletter

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Parent Mental Health workshops
Description:
9 online sessions available for parents and carers;  Supporting your childs mental health 
Managing your own mental health 
Online Safety 
Puberty and Growing Up 
Self-Harm 
Sexual Health 
Child Exploitation 
Healthy Relationships and Consent 
Statutory Relationships and Sex Education
Support Category: Family Support; Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Self harm; Wellbeing

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Peer Ambassador Programme
Description: The child health team offer training courses Secondary school children to be able to support their peers

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Positive Period
Description: Menstrual wellness lesson plan to understand period products and promote menstrual wellbeing. Helping to combat period stigma and promote period positivity and also improve access to period products  info.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: PSHE Network
Description: To bring PSHE colleagues together alongside the LA PSHE lead to ensure information, guidance, resources and training are contemporary. This includes teacher training packs and resources as well as a forum for discussion of methods, successes and other practices.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: PSHE Subject Review
Description: needs assessment mapping current PSHE provision and feedback, guidance and key actions for embed a whole school approach to promote emotional health and wellbeing.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Resilience Programmes
Description: A train the trainer programme that provides the CYPs workforce with the resources and skills that will enable them to deliver a series of workshops aimed at helping CYP to understand what is resilience and develop the skills needed to nurture their resilience

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: RSE
Description: Relationships  Sex education package developed in partnership with schools and the PSHE associate, full scheme of work, resources to enable lesson delivery, resource library, 3 hours of PH staff support per year

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: School Assemblies
Description: Child Health team are able to provide a series of health and wellbeing assemblies. Silver level ABM schools award able to access 3x per year.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Self Harm Awareness Training
Description: Training available to help identify CYP at risk of self harm and improve confidence in discussing concerns with CYP and their family. For parentscarersprofessionals.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Suicide Prevention
Description:
Emotional support and understanding to someone who feels like they cant go on can have a positive effect. If people feel they can ask for help and find it when they need it, then more suicides can be prevented.

Using this online course, you could gain the skills and confidence to help someone who may be considering suicide.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Workplace Health Programme
Description: Helps places to become healthier places to work with different award levels available.

Concat: Public Health Child Health Team: Youth Mental Health First Aid
Description: The child health team offer training courses for those who support those aged 8-18.

This course covers Foetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder. FASD is the leading known preventable intellectual disability and is only caused by prenatal alcohol exposure. International studies have suggested that between 1 and 5 ofpeople have this condition.
Referral Process: See course dates and make a booking via the weblink
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Health; Behaviour

Description: all language mental health support for unaccompanied asylum seekers including group therapy, psycho-social groups and psychoeducational workshops. The approach is flexible, trauma informed and utilises non-confrontational and creative ways of expressing oneself and supporting young people with managing difficult emotions
Support Category: Cultural Identity & Migration Support; Wellbeing; Anxiety/Depression/Low mood

Concat: Royal College of Psychiatrists: RC Psych
Description: This webpage looks at the reasons behind why people self-harm and offers advice about what to do to help.
Referral Process: No referral process, information and advice

Description:
CETRs are focused on those children and young people who either have been, or may be about to be 
admitted to a specialist mental health  learning disability hospital either in the NHS or in the independent 
sector
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Description: Medway's local offer for SEND children and young people
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Description: Network group for school SENCOs
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Description:
The Early Years SEND Team work with a range of children who have been identified as having complex additional needs. 
At one year, if a child is assessed to have physical difficulties, they may be invited to attend Little Builders  a structured programme to assess if a referral to physiotherapy is required. At two years, if a child is assessed to have communication difficulties, they may be invited to attend Big Builders  a structured programme to assess if a referral to speech and language therapy is required. For children with identified complex needs following Big Builders they may be invited to a Targeted Activity Group (TAG) run by the Early years SEND practitioners. SEND practitioners are the named contact for a patch of private, voluntary and independent settings. They offer a termly SENCO surgery and telephone support for the settings when required. Children are usually referred to the team by health visitors or community nursery nurses following a childs ASQ check. In addition we will take referrals from the Childrens Therapy Team and also from parents where appropriate. The team also offer PBS training for parents and nursery age professionals.
Referral Process: Children are usually referred to the team by health visitors or community nursery nurses following a child�s ASQ check. In addition we will take referrals from the Children�s Therapy Team and also from parents where appropriate. Email eyhighneeds@medway.gov.uk
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Family Support; Behaviour

Description: The Special Educational Needs (SEN) Team consider requests for, and co-ordinate, Education, Health and Care (EHC) assessments for children and young people ages 0-25. If the local authority decides that an Education, Health and Care Plan (EHCP) should be issued following assessment, the team also secure educational placement and ensure that provision, as detailed in the EHCP, is in place for the child or young person.
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Description: Protecting bereaved children and young people (CYP) from developing an Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) as a result of a traumatic death. Slide Away is offering specialist support to children and young people to help gain a sense of control and confidence about their future by enabling them to adapt, emotionally and psychologically, to a life that has changed.
Referral Process: How to refer section on website

Concat: The Luna Foundation: Suicide Bereavement Training: Early Years Practitioners
Service: Suicide Bereavement Training: Early Years Practitioners
Description:
Designed for anyone who works with children aged five and under. Developed through international research, lived experience insights and collaboration with early years practitioners.

Our three-hour interactive online session will help you feel more confident supporting young children after suicide bereavement.

There will be opportunities to share experiences and ask questions during interactive elements and breakout sessions.

 Each of our live training sessions can accommodate up to 20 people. Our training is CPD-accredited and participants can claim three hours of professional training time. All participants will receive a certificate of completion and an invitation to become a Luna Ambassador.

The Unstoppable Girls community is all about providing a safe space, a space to be seen  heard, a space to explore neurodiversity and to no longer feel weird or alone. Unstoppable Girls CIC works with teenage and young adult females. This project will design and deliver a course called Confidence and ADHD that will run over 4 weeks. They will deliver this course in a variety of settings within schools, colleges youth hubs, community settings and online. Around the Ashford district.
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Description: Support for children who are in care and Medway is the responsible Local Authority. Some children in care placed at Medway schools and will have a different responsible local authority. Projects Medway Virtual School currently support in school regarding mental health include place2be, mind, Lego therapy and school based counsellors. Each child in care has a personal educational plan (PEP) termly and as part of this are able to request pupil premium funding on an individual level according to their needs. This includes but is not limited to: The Eden project and Lego therapy.
Support Category: Social Care; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Bullying; Trauma/Victim support; Behaviour; Homelessness, finance, employment; Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Wellbeing; Bereavement

Concat: Virtual School: MIND virtual school project
Description: MIND support some of our schools who have a high number of Children in Care by providing a service that is flexible around the needs of the cohort. They can support schools with 1:1 sessions, group work, teacher awareness around certain issues or drop in sessions for pupils. They focus on emotional wellbeing and positive coaching but can also adapt to current themes or topics that are prevalent in school such as anxiety, self-harm, or how to manage stress.

Concat: VRU Kent and Medway: Don't Do Nothing
Description: A campaign for young people to promote the Active Bystander Approach and highlights options which are available to help prevent harm from happening and what they can do to help someone who may be in trouble or in distress without putting themselves at risk.

The post is joint across NELFT  Youth Justice Service to provide triage and signposting support for the Young Offender and brief intervention if required. This service is only available to YOS and external professionals cannot refer into the service.
Referral Process: Must be a Youth Justice Service case already existing.

Description: Youth offending teams work with young people that get into trouble with the law. They look into the background of a young person and try to help them stay away from crime. They also run local crime prevention programmes, help young people at the police station if theyre arrested, help young people and their families at court, supervise young people serving a community sentence and stay in touch with a young person if theyre sentenced to custody.
Referral Process: Referrals made through police or court. In some instances social services may be able to refer dependent on the situation.

Description: Brief Intervention Counselling aimed at young people aged 10-19 are experiencing low level needs or need support with various challenges in their day to day lives. The service will be able to offer up to 6 sessions of counselling per young person, including an assessment session. The service is now open to adult referrals as well.
Age range: Primary school age, Secondary School age and post 16

Concat: Youth service: Youth clubs and activities
Description: A range of activities, support and opportunities for young people aged 8-19 and up to 25 for those with additional needs. Sessions include: sports, music, arts, cookery, social spaces, rock climbing, archery, Duke of Edinburgh Award and more.
Referral Process: Young people can join most sessions without a referral by attending at the advertised time. When otherwise this will be stated.
Age range: Primary school age, Secondary School age and post 16

Description: Amazing Minds is the new and exciting, one-to-one, one hour per week, six session growth mindset programme based on the belief that abilities and intelligence can be developed with effort, learning, courage and persistence.
Referral Process: Schools involved in the project work with the provider and families to select children

Concat: Child Health Commissioning: Child Mental Health Website
Description: We all need a bit of extra help from time to time. Knowing where your child or young person can seek help for their mental wellbeing, should they need it, is always worthwhile. To support you, we've pulled together services in Medway who can support children and young people across the year. There are a range of activities and initiatives available for children across Medway that can help contribute towards good mental health.
Support Category: Anxiety/Depression/Low mood; Wellbeing; Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD; Bereavement; Family Support

Description: e-wellbeing is an online platform offering a range of mental health resources and self-help tools for young people. It aims to provide young people with practical ways to manage their mental well-being and seek help when needed. The website includes interactive activities, videos and information on a variety of mental health topics, including stress, anxiety, and low mood
Source: Kent, Surrey and Sussex: Engaging Young adults with mental health challenges: a Clinician's Guide

Concat: Sussex Partnership NHS Foundation Trust: iRock
Description: iRock is a UK-based service offering youth-friendly mental health support for young people aged 16-25. It provides personalised coaching and therapy services, including a unique blend of mental health
Source: Kent, Surrey and Sussex: Engaging Young adults with mental health challenges: a Clinician's Guide

Concat: Medway Council: Giving Children the Best Start in Life
Service: Giving Children the Best Start in Life
Description: Training that aims to help professionals have effective conversations with parents  carers in order to support healthy pregnancy, child development, positive parenting and general health  wellbeing.  We will also provide resources and signposting information to help families access additional support through Medways Children  Family Hubs  Start for Life offer.

SPOT is Beats online learning platform for school professionals working with pupils in primary, secondary, and further education settings. It's aim is to raise awareness of eating disorders within educational environments and to equip education professionals with the knowledge and confidence to identify early signs of an eating disorder and to signpost pupils and their families to the appropriate medical support. SPOT is currently fully funded for all primary and secondary schools, alongside sixth forms and further education colleges.

Autism Central is for families and carers of autistic people and those who support them. Autism Central is a peer support programme co-produced with parents, carers, and autistic people of all ages. We offers families education, coaching, and connection through resources, one-to-one peer support, and community events.
Support Category: Special Educational Needs and Disability/ Autism/ADHD

Description: Reachable Moments support young victims of youth violence who have been admitted to the Medway Maritime hospital, Medway and William Harvey Hospital, Ashford. We strongly believe that this is a critical moment for these young people, where we can make a real difference by supporting them with intervention at the earliest opportunity. We understand that gang culture is a challenge that young people and our community face and the risks our young people have around county lines, child sexual exploitation, domestic violence, and substance misuse. This work is in partnership with the safeguarding teams at the hospitals and is building relationships with the local police.

MHT and MHT teams provide community-based support and treatment for adults of all ages experiencing mental health challenges. These teams bring together the expertise of the former Community Mental Health Teams (CMHT) and Community Mental Health Services for Older People (CMHSOP) to make sure that care is tailored to individual needs, regardless of age
Referral Process: Professional referral including GP, Talking Therapies, Local Authorities etc.
